title: Board Members: Authority
description: Individual school board members have authority only when acting as part of the full board — no single member can make decisions or take actions on behalf of the district alone. Understanding this helps community members know how board decisions are made and who is accountable for them. When engaging with the board, residents should direct concerns through official channels rather than expecting individual members to act unilaterally.

timeline_local.summary: Revisions to Board Policy BBE (Local) were discussed. No action was taken.

timeline_local.summary: This is a summaries page covering multiple policy changes: (1) BBE(Local): Informed by HB 4310 — requires Board member PIR requests be fulfilled promptly and at no cost; establishes new process for handling requests for potentially confidential information. (2) CH(Local): Informed by SB 12 & 1173 — permits raising aggregate purchasing threshold from $50,000 to $100,000; adds non-discrimination clause for HUBs. (3) CKC(Local): Informed by HB 121 — requires district emergency plan to include provisions for safety of students, staff, and spectators during District-sponsored/sanctioned extracurricular activities. (4) CKE(Local): Informed by HB 1458 — adds process for Board to authorize use of reserve police officers, with new section on procedures, authority, standards, and limitations. (5) CL(Local): Informed by SB 8 ('bathroom bill') — defines multiple-occupancy private spaces, requires sex-designated signage, outlines exceptions (young children, those needing assistance), requires facility use aligned with sex at birth, adds locker room as instructional classroom section, and outlines violation process.
changes_added: BBE(Local): Board member PIR requests must be fulfilled promptly and at no cost
BBE(Local): New process for handling requests for potentially confidential information
CH(Local): Aggregate purchasing threshold increase from $50,000 to $100,000 (optional for districts)
CH(Local): Non-discrimination clause for HUBs
CKC(Local): Emergency plan must include provisions for safety of students, staff, and spectators during District-sponsored or District-sanctioned extracurricular activities
CKE(Local): New policy section outlining process for Board authorization of reserve police officers, including procedures, authority, standards, and limitations
CL(Local): Definition of multiple-occupancy private spaces
CL(Local): Requirement for appropriate signage consistent with sex designated for each space
CL(Local): Specific exceptions (young children, individuals requiring assistance)
CL(Local): Requirement to use facilities aligned with sex at birth
CL(Local): New section addressing use of locker rooms as instructional classrooms
CL(Local): Process for violations of the policy
The District may request a Board member who is receiving public information, that is confidential under law, to sign a confidentiality agreement that covers the information and requires that:
1. The information not be disclosed;
2. The information be labeled as confidential;
3. The information be kept securely; or

4. The number of copies made of the information or the notes taken from the information that implicate the confidential nature of the information be controlled, with all copies or notes that are not destroyed or returned remaining confidential and subject to the confidentiality agreement.

A Board member who has received a request to sign a confidentiality agreement may seek a decision, from the Attorney General, about whether the information covered by the confidentiality agreement is confidential under law.
Public information that is confidential under law shall be redacted from the information provided to the Board member without charge.

Information subject to attorney-client privilege is not subject to disclosure to a member of a Board member unless the attorney-client relationship upon which the privilege is based applies to the member. The district shall inform the Board member if information responsive to a request is withheld due to attorney-client privilege.
Public information requested in compliance with law and this policy shall be provided promptly and without charge.

description_local: This policy defines the authority and decision-making power of individual school board members and the board as a whole. It clarifies that official authority rests with the full board acting together, not with any single member acting alone, and outlines the limited circumstances in which an individual member may act on behalf of the district. Parents and community members should understand this policy because it helps explain how board decisions are made, who has the power to speak or act for the district, and why requests or concerns are typically directed to the full board rather than to individual members.
